channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Wheelchair users say private equity is making repairs harder
item.description: People who rely on wheelchairs say that industry consolidation driven by private equity means long delays in getting them fixed, which isolates them from society and endangers their health.
item.content:encoded: People who rely on wheelchairs say that industry consolidation driven by private equity means long delays in getting them fixed, which isolates them from society and endangers their health.
item.dc:creator: Meghan H Smith

item.title: When resources run thin, volunteer ranchers fight local fires
item.description: Rangeland Fire Protection Districts are volunteer groups made up of neighbors who protect their property and nearby land from wildfires, especially when federal and state resources are stretched thin.
item.content:encoded: Rangeland Fire Protection Districts are volunteer groups made up of neighbors who protect their property and nearby land from wildfires, especially when federal and state resources are stretched thin.
item.dc:creator: Alejandro Figueroa

item.title: How climate change is worsening heat in Europe
item.description: Scott Detrow speaks with extreme weather researcher Theodore Keeping about Europe's hottest heatwave. With over 1,000 deaths in France alone, lives depend on the EU's response to rising temperatures.
item.content:encoded: Scott Detrow speaks with extreme weather researcher Theodore Keeping about Europe's hottest heatwave. With over 1,000 deaths in France alone, lives depend on the EU's response to rising temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Fio Geiran

item.title: Mother of murdered sailor demands Navy change how it handles sexual assault cases
item.description: The mother of a murdered sailor is demanding the Navy to make long-term changes after her daughter died at the hands of a shipmate.
item.content:encoded: The mother of a murdered sailor is demanding the Navy to make long-term changes after her daughter died at the hands of a shipmate.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: Supreme Court takes sledgehammer to much of federal government's regulatory structure
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down most of the limits that Congress and the courts had previously established to protect the independence of regulatory agencies that comprise much of the federal government.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court struck down most of the limits that Congress and the courts had previously established to protect the independence of regulatory agencies that comprise much of the federal government.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: How a stranger helped a man falling on the subway without damaging his dignity
item.description: When Lia Eastep was 21, her father had a stroke which left him unsteady on his feet. His physical limitations almost caused an accident on a subway, but a stranger stepped in.
item.content:encoded: When Lia Eastep was 21, her father had a stroke which left him unsteady on his feet. His physical limitations almost caused an accident on a subway, but a stranger stepped in.

item.title: A small mahogany desk with a 250-year history
item.description: Thomas Jefferson drafted the Declaration of Independence on a small, portable desk 250 years ago. Today, it's on display in the Smithsonian.
item.content:encoded: Thomas Jefferson drafted the Declaration of Independence on a small, portable desk 250 years ago. Today, it's on display in the Smithsonian.
item.dc:creator: Clare Lombardo

item.title: Serena and Venus Williams reunite at Wimbledon
item.description: Serena Williams, age 44, is returning to Wimbledon to play both singles and doubles alongside her sister Venus Williams.
item.content:encoded: Serena Williams, age 44, is returning to Wimbledon to play both singles and doubles alongside her sister Venus Williams.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: Here to Help: Jennifer Timmick guides visitors through the stories of American history
item.description: For our series Here to Help, we speak with Jennifer Timmick, volunteer tour guide at the National Museum of American History. She talks about the stories of ordinary people who did extraordinary things.
item.content:encoded: For our series Here to Help, we speak with Jennifer Timmick, volunteer tour guide at the National Museum of American History. She talks about the stories of ordinary people who did extraordinary things.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: Minneapolis Fed president reacts to Supreme Court ruling
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Neel Kashkari, president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis, following the Supreme Court ruling preventing President Trump from firing Fed governor Lisa Cook.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Neel Kashkari, president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis, following the Supreme Court ruling preventing President Trump from firing Fed governor Lisa Cook.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Acovino
